rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH GARZA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker Llps Motion To Quash Notice Of Levy
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Elderman & Dicker LLP¿s motion to quash notice of levy is denied. As much as the court finds it distasteful for plaintiffs/judgment creditors to serve a notice of levy on attorneys for a defendant/judgment debtor, nothing in the enforcement of judgment statutes precludes judgment creditors from doing so. Wilson Elser¿s assertion of the attorney-client privilege is unfounded since disclosing whether it has money or properly belonging to ACL in its possession is not the type of subject that is covered by the privilege. (Evidence Code §952). Plaintiffs¿ request for attorneys¿ fee is denied because the issue raised by the motion is novel and the court has no reason to believe that Wilson Elser acted for a wrongful purpose in bringing the motion. Added on calendar: Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP¿s motion to stay enforcement is granted in part and denied in part. Absent further order of the court upon application made the plaintiffs, any enforcement of judgment efforts that plaintiffs may make that are directed to Wilson Elser are limited to ascertaining whether Wilson Elser has money or properly belonging to ACL in its possession and, if so, levying on that money or property. The court has the authority, and exercises that authority, to protect Wilson Elser and its attorneys and staff from any harassing or burdensome judgment enforcement efforts, That said, based on the papers filed, the court neither finds nor assumes that plaintiffs or their counsel intend to do anything that can fairly be characterized as harassing or burdensome. = (220/HEK)
